title: Group Accountant
description:
A well-established property group with operations in Singapore, China, and Australia is seeking a versatile, dynamic, and responsible professional to join our team as Group Accountant, reporting directly to the Group Financial Controller.

Key Responsibilities

Financial Forecasting & Budgeting

·       Develop, maintain, and enhance financial forecasting models across multiple business units.

·       Coordinate and lead the annual budgeting process, ensuring alignment with strategic goals and timelines.

Financial Analysis & Reporting

·       Perform ad-hoc financial analysis to support business decisions and strategic initiatives.

·       Prepare monthly group consolidation and management reporting packages.

Audit & Compliance

·       Assist in preparing year-end group audit schedules and consolidation deliverables.

·       Manage year-end audits for all entities, ensuring compliance with internal policies and regulatory standards.

·       Liaise with internal and external auditors, legal counsel, and tax advisors as needed.

Technical Accounting & Projects

·       Support the Financial Controller on technical accounting matters and policy implementation.

·       Contribute to cross-functional projects and business initiatives as required.

Group GST Management

·       Oversee the preparation and submission of GST returns for all entities within the group.

·       Ensure timely and accurate reconciliation of GST accounts and compliance with local tax regulations.

·       Monitor changes in GST legislation and assess their impact on group operations.

·       Coordinate with tax advisors and regulatory bodies on GST-related matters.

·       Provide guidance and training to internal teams on GST processes and compliance requirements.

Requirements:

·       Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or a recognized Professional Accounting Qualification.

·       Minimum 5 years of relevant experience in accounting or finance, preferably in a group or regional role.

·       Strong technical knowledge in group consolidation and financial reporting.

·       Proficient in Microsoft Office; experience with SQL-based accounting systems is preferred.

·       Excellent communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills.

·       Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to work independently or collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.

postedCompany.name: The Management Corporation - Strata Title Plan No. 1455
